frictions.detail: This project still mixes packaged bundles into editable solution space. The preferred bundle output is artifacts/solutions/core.zip, but the current layout tolerates inline solution zips.
frictions.title: Recommended bundle placement is still not canonical everywhere

frictions.detail: This feedback can now restate the route as dev -> dev / CoreDev; prod -> prod / CoreManaged, but operators still need the same stage-to-environment-to-solution wording in other project diagnostics.
frictions.title: Adjacent commands still need the same mapping explanation

frictions.detail: The canonical bundle path is artifacts/solutions/core.zip, but that zip is a generated artifact and may be absent until you pack local source from solutions or export the solution from Dataverse. Typical creation paths are pp solution pack <solution-folder> --out artifacts/solutions/core.zip or pp solution export CoreManaged --environment prod --out artifacts/solutions/core.zip.
frictions.title: Bundle artifact lifecycle is still easy to miss

frictions.detail: Stage prod in pp.config.yaml selects environment alias prod. The alias name lives in the project config, but the actual Dataverse URL and auth profile are resolved later from the external pp environment registry.
frictions.title: Environment alias provenance is still easy to miss

frictions.detail: The local model is structurally coherent, but these required parameters still block a fully deployable project: sqlEndpoint.
frictions.title: Required inputs remain unresolved

recommendedTasks.rationale: Agents still have to infer whether inline solution bundles are merely supported or actually recommended.
recommendedTasks.title: Tighten layout validation around canonical bundle output

recommendedTasks.rationale: Operators should not have to translate between feedback output and other commands to explain how a stage resolves to an environment alias and solution target.
recommendedTasks.title: Keep stage-to-environment-to-solution wording aligned across project diagnostics

recommendedTasks.rationale: Operators should see whether the bundle path is already populated and which pack or export action is expected to create it.
recommendedTasks.title: Explain when canonical bundle artifacts should exist

recommendedTasks.rationale: The project stores alias names locally, but operators still need to know that live environment resolution comes from the external pp environment registry and auth profiles.
recommendedTasks.title: Explain environment alias provenance alongside active targets

summary.activeTargetSummary: stage prod -> environment prod -> solution CoreManaged
summary.bundleLifecycleSummary: The canonical bundle path is artifacts/solutions/core.zip, but that zip is a generated artifact and may be absent until you pack local source from solutions or export the solution from Dataverse. Typical creation paths are pp solution pack <solution-folder> --out artifacts/solutions/core.zip or pp solution export CoreManaged --environment prod --out artifacts/solutions/core.zip.
summary.environmentAliasProvenance: Stage prod in pp.config.yaml selects environment alias prod. The alias name lives in the project config, but the actual Dataverse URL and auth profile are resolved later from the external pp environment registry.

workflowWins.detail: The local project model resolves through pp.config.yaml, which keeps the repo contract anchored in one place.
workflowWins.title: Single config anchor

workflowWins.detail: Editable assets stay under apps, flows, docs, while packaged solution bundles are expected at artifacts/solutions/core.zip.
workflowWins.title: Source-first asset contract

workflowWins.detail: The project already explains the current deployment route as stage prod -> environment prod -> solution CoreManaged.
workflowWins.title: Stage-to-environment-to-solution mapping
